extra.reasoning: The property requires that setting hemoptysis=true (with all other inputs fixed) never lowers acuity relative to hemoptysis=false. I examined all 12 rules (r1-r12) and the default. None of the rule conditions reference the hemoptysis input — the rules trigger only on vision_loss, fever, vomiting, palpitations, leg_swelling, chest_pain, dyspnea, pallor, confusion, stiff_neck, focal_weakness, and abdominal_pain. Because hemoptysis appears in no rule condition (and is not the output), toggling it has zero effect on which rules match, on priority/tie resolution, or on the default. For any fixed assignment of the other 12 inputs, the computed acuity is identical whether hemoptysis is false or true. Identical output is trivially 'no lower than before,' so the monotonicity property holds across the entire input space. No counterexample exists.
